cb_step_1.title: Select the pelvic CT volume
cb_step_1.simple: Choose the pelvic CT volume from the list.
cb_step_1.detailed: This step identifies the patient's pelvic CT scan that will be used for all subsequent segmentation and planning. Select the appropriate volume from the 'Input CT Volume' dropdown.

cb_step_2.title: Segment the pelvis
cb_step_2.simple: Click 'Run Step 1: Segment Pelvis' to begin automatic pelvic bone segmentation.
cb_step_2.detailed: This step automatically segments the pelvic bone from the selected CT volume. The resulting segmentation provides the anatomical reference for fracture identification and template generation.

cb_step_3.title: Segment the fractures
cb_step_3.simple: Click 'Run Step 2: Segment Fractures' to detect and label fracture fragments.
cb_step_3.detailed: This step identifies and labels individual fracture fragments from the pelvic segmentation. Accurate fragment segmentation is essential for planning reduction and screw placement.

cb_step_4.title: Exclude unwanted fragments
cb_step_4.simple: In the fragment list, untick any fragments you want to exclude from planning.
cb_step_4.detailed: You can exclude small or non-critical fragments that do not require surgical fixation. Only fragments that remain ticked will be used in the reduction and screw planning steps. Untick any fragments that should be ignored.

cb_step_5.title: Generate reduction template
cb_step_5.simple: Click 'Run Step 3: Generate Template' to create a template for fracture reduction.
cb_step_5.detailed: This step generates a template model that guides the virtual reduction of the fracture fragments back to their anatomical position. The template is used in the next step to compute the reduction transformation.

cb_step_6.title: Register and reduce fragments
cb_step_6.simple: Click 'Run Step 4: Register _Reduce' to perform automatic reduction of the fracture.
cb_step_6.detailed: This step automatically aligns the fracture fragments to their correct anatomical positions using the generated template. The result is a reduced pelvic model that simulates the post-operative alignment.

cb_step_7.title: Decide on manual adjustments
cb_step_7.simple: If further fragment adjustments are needed, tick the 'Manually adjust a fragment' checkbox. Otherwise, skip to step 12.
cb_step_7.detailed: The automatic reduction may not be perfect. You can choose to manually fine-tune individual fragments. If the reduction looks satisfactory, proceed directly to screw planning.

cb_step_8.title: Choose a fragment to adjust
cb_step_8.simple: From the 'Fragment' dropdown, select the fragment you wish to adjust.
cb_step_8.detailed: Pick the specific fracture fragment whose position and rotation need manual correction. Only one fragment can be adjusted at a time.

cb_step_9.title: Adjust fragment position and rotation
cb_step_9.simple: Use the transform controls to move and rotate the selected fragment into the desired position.
cb_step_9.detailed: Manually reposition the fragment by dragging the transform handles in the 3D view. Fine adjustments help achieve optimal alignment before finalizing the reduction.

cb_step_10.title: Further adjustments needed?
cb_step_10.simple: If more adjustments are required for this fragment, choose 'Yes, adjust more' to continue. Otherwise, choose 'No, apply adjustments'.
cb_step_10.detailed: You can iterate on the position and rotation of the fragment until it fits correctly. When satisfied, proceed to apply the adjustments to lock in the changes.

cb_step_11.title: Apply adjustments
cb_step_11.simple: Click the 'Apply adjustments' button to finalize the manual changes.
cb_step_11.detailed: This step saves the manual position and rotation changes you made, updating the reduced model with your modifications. The fragment is now locked in its adjusted location.

cb_step_12.title: Plan screw trajectories
cb_step_12.simple: Click 'Run Step 5: Plan Screws' to automatically generate screw placement trajectories.
cb_step_12.detailed: This step plans optimal screw paths for fracture fixation based on the reduced pelvic model. The generated screw models show recommended trajectories for surgical fixation.

cb_step_13.title: Decide on screw trajectory edits
cb_step_13.simple: If you want to adjust the automatically planned screw trajectories, tick the 'Edit Screw trajectories' checkbox. Otherwise, the workflow is complete.
cb_step_13.detailed: The automatic screw plan may need refinement. You can manually edit the trajectories to better suit the patient's anatomy or surgical approach. If the plan is acceptable, the workflow ends here.

cb_step_14.title: Adjust screw trajectories
cb_step_14.simple: Manually adjust the position and rotation of each screw trajectory in the 3D view.
cb_step_14.detailed: Use the transform controls to fine-tune the screw paths. Ensure each trajectory avoids critical structures and provides adequate fixation per the surgical plan.

cb_step_15.title: Regenerate screws from edited lines
cb_step_15.simple: Click 'Regenerate screws from edited lines' to update the screw models with your changes.
cb_step_15.detailed: This step applies your manual trajectory edits, generating updated screw models that reflect the adjusted paths. The final screw models are now ready for surgical reference.

cb_step_16.title: Finalize screw editing
cb_step_16.simple: Untick the 'Edit Screw trajectories' checkbox to exit editing mode.
cb_step_16.detailed: This step returns the module to its standard state, confirming that screw editing is complete. The final screw plan is now ready for export or review.
